title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: Conceptually: triangle preserves cohort axis (policies underwritten) development axis (loss accrues development time). canonical chain-ladder data structure. calendar collapses cohorts onto diagonal — row one calendar period across underwriting cohorts. Equivalent diagonal sum triangle. total collapses dimensions one value per group. Useful portfolio-level comparison (product worst loss ratio window?).

title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: row one (cohort, dev) cell cumulative loss / risk premium. Visualise line plot heatmap: Use triangle input : - build_ata(), build_ed() — development factors - fit_cl(), fit_lr() — projection - detect_cohort_regime() — structural change detection
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp <- as_experience(experience)  tri <- build_triangle(exp, group_var = cv_nm) head(tri) #>    cv_nm n_obs     cohort dev   loss     rp  closs    crp ... #> 1:   SUR    30 2023-04-01       1      0  ...       0  ... #> 2:   2CI    30 2023-04-01       1      0  ...       0  ... #> ... plot(tri)              # one trajectory per cohort, faceted by group plot_triangle(tri)     # cohort × dev heatmap of clr

title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: row one calendar period (per group). dev column sequential index (1, 2, 3, …) within group, “development period since cohort start”. Calendar aggregation mathematically diagonal sum triangle: cells cym (regardless uym/elap_m) combined. Use cases: - Trend analysis (“loss ratio rising calendar time”) - Calendar-year effect detection (e.g., regulatory shock, premium -leveling event) - Portfolio monitoring dashboards
code: cal <- build_calendar(exp, group_var = cv_nm, calendar_var = "cym") head(cal) #>    cv_nm   calendar dev   loss     rp  closs    crp ... #> 1:   SUR 2023-04-01       1      0  ...       0  ... #> 2:   SUR 2023-05-01       2      0  ...       0  ... #> ... plot(cal)                           # x = calendar plot(cal, x_by = "dev")         # x = sequential index

title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: One row per group, summarising loss / risk premium / loss ratio window. period_from / period_to arguments restrict fixed window groups comparable. Use cases: - Compare overall loss ratio across coverages - Rank groups reserve / share portfolio - Build executive summary tables
code: tot <- build_total(   exp,   group_var = cv_nm,   cohort_var = "uym",   period_from = "2023-04-01",   period_to   = "2024-03-01" ) head(tot) #>    cv_nm n_obs sales_start sales_end       loss          rp        lr ... #> 1:   SUR    12  2023-04-01 2024-03-01  ...        ...       ...

title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: three start experience aggregate demographic dimensions away. Choose framework based analytical question.
code: experience (long, with demographics)                               │          ┌────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐          │                    │                     │    build_triangle      build_calendar         build_total    (cohort × dev)      (calendar series)     (portfolio total)          │                    │                     │          ▼                    ▼                     ▼      triangle             calendar               total    (2D, projection)     (1D, trend)         (0D, comparison)

title: Aggregation frameworks: triangle, calendar, total
text: aggregation, object stores source-column metadata attributes (used plot labels granularity-aware date formatting): data columns standardised cohort / dev / calendar, downstream code granularity-agnostic.
code: attr(tri, "cohort_var")      # "uym" attr(tri, "cohort_type")     # "month" attr(tri, "dev_var")     # "elap_m" attr(tri, "dev_type")    # "month"  attr(cal, "calendar_var")    # "cym" attr(cal, "calendar_type")   # "month"

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: Reserving projection methods fitted observed data, practical value lies performed past valuation dates. backtest() answers question hiding latest holdout calendar diagonals triangle, refitting model earlier portion, comparing projection actuals withheld. calendar-diagonal hold-(rather dev-period hold-), simulates “model said K months ago valuation date?”. cell-level metric Actual-Expected Gap, aeg=vpred/vactual−1\mathrm{aeg} = v_{\mathrm{pred}} / v_{\mathrm{actual}} - 1, positive values flag -projection negative values flag -projection.

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: returned object "backtest" list key slots: aeg — per-cell data.table (cohort, dev, actual, pred, aeg, calendar_idx). col_summary — AEG aggregated dev. diag_summary — AEG aggregated calendar diagonal. masked — triangle fit trained (latest diagonals removed). fit — fit object returned fit_fn (cl_fit lr_fit). summary(bt) prints two summary tables alongside call metadata.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp     <- as_experience(experience) tri_sur <- build_triangle(exp[cv_nm == "SUR"], cv_nm)  bt <- backtest(tri_sur, holdout = 6L, value_var = "closs", method = "mack") print(bt) #> <backtest> #>   fit_fn      : fit_cl #>   value_var   : closs #>   holdout     : 6 calendar diagonals #>   held-out    : 123 cells #>   AEG         : mean -4.83% / median -3.18%

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: Removing latest holdout diagonals shortens lower-right edge triangle. chain ladder fit masked triangle can project far longest cohort × dev support; cells beyond support, belong oldest cohorts largest development periods, simply projection compare . function silently filters unreachable cells, bt$aeg always contains cells actual finite projection exist. practical takeaway: holdout larger diagonals reduces validation set fastest oldest cohorts, chain ladder otherwise rely extrapolated tail.

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: col_summary — systematic bias development period. consistently signed AEG given dev signals structural mismatch model maturity. Early-dev positive values usually reflect inflated link factors; late-dev values flag tail miscalibration. aeg_mean averages cell-level AEG, aeg_med median, aeg_wt = sum(pred - actual) / sum(actual) exposure-weighted. Comparing three columns flags whether large cells dominate (aeg_wt different aeg_med) bias uniform. diag_summary — calendar-year effect. single bad diagonal otherwise unbiased output points calendar event (rate change, claim handling shift, one-shock) static chain ladder see construction. monotone drift across calendar diagonals (SUR example , AEG becomes increasingly negative across 25, ..., 30) typically indicates latest period running better earlier-cohort link factors imply. aeg — cell-level outliers. diagnosing specific cohort × dev cells, inspect bt$aeg directly:

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: Four plot views registered "backtest": type = "col" right place look systematic dev-period bias; type = "diag" reveals calendar-year drift; type = "cell" exposes cohorts contribute bias; plot_triangle() puts cell-level AEG values triangular layout plot_triangle() underlying fit, red/blue diverging palette red marks -projection.
code: plot(bt, type = "col")    # AEG by dev (point + dashed zero line) plot(bt, type = "diag")   # AEG by calendar diagonal plot(bt, type = "cell")   # per-cohort AEG trajectories over dev plot_triangle(bt)         # diverging-color heatmap on the held-out wedge

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: Choose holdout balance two opposing effects: large: masked triangle loses latest experience, oldest cohorts reachable cells later dev periods. validation set shrinks unevenly, biased toward early dev. small: held-wedge just thin diagonal band, may capture enough cells reveal systematic patterns. Typical choices holdout = 6L (half-year) monthly triangles, holdout = 12L (full year) stronger validation triangle least 24–30 diagonals history.

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: backtest() supports fit_cl fit_lr. fitter passed fit_fn, value_var selects projection column fit$full compare held-actuals. fit_cl, value_var also forwarded fit . fit_lr — projects loss exposure jointly — value_var chooses comparison column, mapping: Backtesting clr often informative diagnostic: loss ratio unitless dimension-free across cohorts different volume, aeg_mean aeg_med carry consistent meaning across triangle. Backtesting closs weights result toward whichever cohorts happen largest held-diagonals.
code: bt_cl  <- backtest(tri_sur, holdout = 6L, fit_fn = fit_cl,                    value_var = "closs", method = "mack") bt_lr  <- backtest(tri_sur, holdout = 6L, fit_fn = fit_lr,                    method = "sa", value_var = "closs") bt_clr <- backtest(tri_sur, holdout = 6L, fit_fn = fit_lr,                    method = "sa", value_var = "clr")  print(bt_clr) #> <backtest> #>   fit_fn      : fit_lr #>   value_var   : clr #>   holdout     : 6 calendar diagonals #>   held-out    : 123 cells #>   AEG         : mean 1.25% / median -0.22%

title: Backtesting projections against held-out diagonals
text: vignette("chain-ladder") — fit_cl() reference. vignette("loss-ratio-methods") — fit_lr() "sa", "ed", "cl" methods. ?backtest, ?plot.backtest, ?plot_triangle.backtest.

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: value_var selects cumulative column project — typically "closs" (cumulative loss) reserving, "crp" (cumulative risk premium) exposure projection.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp <- as_experience(experience) tri <- build_triangle(exp, group_var = cv_nm)  cl <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack") print(cl)

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: Two estimation methods available: method = "mack" enables projection plot’s confidence bands (show_interval = TRUE):
code: cl_basic <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "basic") cl_mack  <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack")  names(cl_basic) #> [1] "call" "data" "method" "group_var" "cohort_var" "dev_var" "value_var" #>     "full" "pred" "ata" "summary" "factor" "selected" "maturity" #>     "alpha" "sigma_method" "weight_var" "recent" "use_maturity" #>     "maturity_args" "tail" "tail_factor"  # Mack adds variance estimates to $full and $summary head(cl_mack$summary) #>    cv_nm     cohort latest ultimate reserve   proc_se   param_se        se        cv #> 1:  ... plot(cl_mack, type = "projection", show_interval = TRUE)

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: triangles latest observed development period still developing, extrapolated tail factor estimates ultimate: extrapolation fits log⁡(fk−1)∼k\log(f_k - 1) \sim k projected factors extends projection cumulative product extrapolated fkf_k values. Disabled default (tail = FALSE).
code: # Log-linear extrapolation from the selected ata factors cl_tail <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack", tail = TRUE)  # Or supply a literal tail factor cl_tail <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack", tail = 1.025)

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: selected ata factors volatile, restrict projection mature region : maturity_args forwarded find_ata_maturity().
code: cl_mat <- fit_cl(   tri,   value_var     = "closs",   method        = "mack",   maturity_args = list(cv_threshold = 0.10, rse_threshold = 0.05) )  cl_mat$maturity #> per-group maturity index (selected ata link from which projection uses CL)

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: fit_cl(method = "mack") decomposes projection variance : proc_se — process variance, σk2\sigma^2_k (residual link variance per development period). param_se — parameter variance, uncertainty selected age--age factors f̂k\hat{f}_k. se — total standard error, proc_se2+param_se2\sqrt{\mathrm{proc\_se}^2 + \mathrm{param\_se}^2}. cv — coefficient variation, se / value_proj.
code: summary(cl_mack) #>    cv_nm     cohort   latest  ultimate  reserve  proc_se param_se       se      cv #> 1:   ...                                  ...      ...      ...     ...      ...

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: type = "reserve" shows reserve per cohort optional error bars (Mack ):
code: plot(cl_mack, type = "reserve", conf_level = 0.95)

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: plot_triangle() displays cohort × dev cells heatmap, distinguishing observed cells projected: label_style = "cv" mode shows coefficient variation per cell, useful spotting unreliable cells:
code: plot_triangle(cl_mack, what = "full")    # observed + projected plot_triangle(cl_mack, what = "pred")    # projected only plot_triangle(cl_mack, what = "data")    # observed only plot_triangle(cl_mack, label_style = "cv") plot_triangle(cl_mack, label_style = "se") plot_triangle(cl_mack, label_style = "ci")

title: Chain ladder reserving with fit_cl
text: Mack variance requires σk\sigma_k development links, including last estimated directly. sigma_method controls extrapolation:
code: fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack", sigma_method = "loglinear")

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: lossratio consumes long-format experience data — one row per (cohort × dev × demographic) cell. bundled dataset experience 33,381-row table calendar / underwriting period columns multiple granularities, demographic dimensions (cv_nm, age_band, gender), amounts (loss, rp).
code: library(lossratio)  data(experience) str(experience) #> Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame':  33381 obs. of  17 variables: #>  $ cy, cyh, cyq, cym  : Date  (calendar period) #>  $ uy, uyh, uyq, uym  : Date  (underwriting period) #>  $ elap_y, elap_h, elap_q, elap_m : int  (elapsed period) #>  $ cv_nm    : chr   (coverage) #>  $ age_band : Ord.factor #>  $ gender   : Factor #>  $ loss, rp : num

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: as_experience() checks required columns (cym, uym, loss, rp), coerces date columns, tags class. Use check_experience(df) non-mutating check.
code: exp <- as_experience(experience) class(exp) #> [1] "experience" "data.table" "data.frame"

what: Step 2 — Build the cohort × dev structure
title: Getting started with lossratio
text: build_triangle(): aggregates demographic dimensions away (: age_band, gender), adds cumulative columns (closs, crp), adds derived metrics (margin, lr, clr, proportions), standardises cohort / development columns names cohort dev, preserves original column names attributes (cohort_var, dev_var) downstream plot labels.
code: tri <- build_triangle(exp, group_var = cv_nm) class(tri) #> [1] "triangle" "data.table" "data.frame" names(tri) #> [1] "cv_nm" "n_obs" "cohort" "dev" "loss" "rp" "closs" "crp" #> [9] "margin" "cmargin" "profit" "cprofit" "lr" "clr" #> [...] proportions

title: Getting started with lossratio
code: plot(tri)              # cohort trajectories of clr plot_triangle(tri)     # heatmap of clr cells summary(tri)           # group-wise statistics by dev

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: Two complementary views cohort development: fit_ata() returns selected age--age factors per development link. fit_ed() returns intensity factors gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k. inputs projection methods .
code: # Age-to-age factors ata <- build_ata(tri, value_var = "closs") fit_ata(ata)  # Exposure-driven intensities ed <- build_ed(tri, loss_var = "closs", exposure_var = "crp") fit_ed(ed)

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_cl() runs chain ladder projection: fit_lr() runs loss-ratio projection. default method = "sa" (stage-adaptive) uses exposure-driven maturity chain ladder , switching per-group maturity point detected ata factors.
code: cl <- fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", method = "mack") plot(cl, type = "projection") summary(cl) lr <- fit_lr(tri, method = "sa") plot(lr, type = "clr") summary(lr)

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: detect_cohort_regime() checks whether recent cohorts behave differently earlier ones. Useful preprocessing step loss-ratio fitting homogeneous subset: See vignette("regime-detection") dedicated walkthrough.
code: sub <- build_triangle(exp[cv_nm == "SUR"], group_var = cv_nm) detect_cohort_regime(sub, K = 12, method = "ecp")

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: vignette("aggregation-frameworks") — use build_triangle vs build_calendar vs build_total. vignette("loss-ratio-methods") — choosing among "sa" / "ed" / "cl" fit_lr(). vignette("chain-ladder") — fit_cl() deep dive (Mack variance, tail factor). vignette("triangle-diagnostics") — summary_ata(), find_ata_maturity(), triangle-style visualisations. vignette("regime-detection") — detect_cohort_regime() structural-change diagnosis across cohorts. vignette("backtest") — calendar-diagonal hold-validation fit_cl fit_lr.

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: cohort ii dev kk: Ci,kLC^L_{,k} — cumulative loss Ci,kPC^P_{,k} — cumulative risk premium (exposure) fk=Ck+1L/CkLf_k = C^L_{k+1} / C^L_k — age--age (chain ladder) factor gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k — exposure-driven intensity maturity point mgm_g — dev fkf_k stabilises group gg (detected CV / RSE thresholds)

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: default method exploits fact fkf_k volatile early stable late, gkg_k behaves opposite way. SA switches estimators maturity point: Ĉ,k+1L={Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kPk<mg(ED maturity)fk⋅Ĉ,kLk≥mg(CL maturity) \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} \;=\; \begin{cases} \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} & k < m_g \quad \text{(ED maturity)} \\ f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k}              & k \ge m_g \quad \text{(CL maturity)} \end{cases} Behaviour: maturity: anchors loss estimate premium volume. Avoids volatile-link explosion classical CL suffers early fkf_k noisy. maturity: preserves cohort’s observed level. Avoids “cohorts converge average” behaviour pure ED suffers tail. use: Long-tail products development extends across many years. Recent cohorts (immature data) mixed older cohorts (matured). Health insurance cohorts structural pre-/post-maturity difference (e.g. waiting period transitions).
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp <- as_experience(experience) tri <- build_triangle(exp, group_var = cv_nm)  lr_sa <- fit_lr(tri, method = "sa")        # default plot(lr_sa, type = "clr") summary(lr_sa)

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: future increments use ED: Ĉ,k+1L=Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kP \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} Behaviour: Stable premium volume informative across full development. Loses cohort-specific level signal — cohorts higher observed loss converge toward group-level gkg_k. use: Short-tail products chain ladder offers advantage. Sparse data age--age factors unreliable across links. Comparing SA / CL sanity check.
code: lr_ed <- fit_lr(tri, method = "ed") plot(lr_ed, type = "clr")

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: Classical Mack model: Ĉ,k+1L=fk⋅Ĉ,kL \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k} Behaviour: Standard reserving practice. Equivalent fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs") loss projection, fit_lr() additionally projects exposure forward via CL crp computes loss-ratio uncertainty via delta method. Volatile early fkf_k noisy — small denominators amplify link errors. use: Mature, stable portfolios age--age factors well-behaved across full development. Reserving exercises regulators expect classical Mack form documentation.
code: lr_cl <- fit_lr(tri, method = "cl") plot(lr_cl, type = "clr")

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
code: lrs <- list(   sa = fit_lr(tri, method = "sa"),   ed = fit_lr(tri, method = "ed"),   cl = fit_lr(tri, method = "cl") )  # Cohort-level summary summary(lrs$sa)$ultimate summary(lrs$ed)$ultimate summary(lrs$cl)$ultimate

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: fit_lr() reports analytical standard errors via delta method. Two delta variants: delta_method = "simple" (default) — treats exposure fixed, SE(L/E)≈SE(L)/E\mathrm{SE}(L/E) \approx \mathrm{SE}(L)/E. delta_method = "full" — accounts exposure uncertainty loss-exposure correlation rho: Var(L/E)≈Var(L)E2+L2Var(E)E4−2ρLSE(L)SE(E)E3 \mathrm{Var}(L/E) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{E^2}   + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(E)}{E^4}   - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(E)}{E^3} Bootstrap intervals also available:
code: lr_boot <- fit_lr(tri, method = "sa", bootstrap = TRUE, B = 1000, seed = 1) summary(lr_boot)

title: Loss-ratio projection methods: SA, ED, CL
text: Quick decision flow: practice: start "sa" (default), run "cl" "ed" sensitivity. three agree, projection robust. diverge, inspect maturity detection underlying ata factors.
code: Is the portfolio fully matured (all cohorts past maturity)?   ├── Yes  →  "cl" (classical, regulator-friendly)   └── No         ├── Are early age-to-age factors volatile?         │     ├── Yes  →  "sa" (default — exposure-driven smoothing)         │     └── No   →  "cl"         └── Is exposure (rp) the more informative signal?               ├── Yes  →  "ed"               └── No   →  "sa"

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
text: analysing portfolio long-duration health insurance cohorts, practitioner often asks two questions: recent underwriting cohorts behaving differently earlier cohorts? , change happen? visual inspection plot(tri_sur) can suggest recent cohorts lower early loss ratios older ones, eye-balling bundle trajectories unreliable way locate structural shift — especially observation windows differ across cohorts. detect_cohort_regime() answers questions one call. treats underwriting cohort feature vector (trajectory development periods 1, ..., K), orders cohorts underwriting date, applies change-point clustering method resulting multivariate sequence.

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
code: library(lossratio)  data(experience) exp     <- as_experience(experience) tri_sur <- build_triangle(exp[cv_nm == "SUR"], cv_nm)

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
text: default method "ecp", non-parametric multivariate change-point algorithm determines number regimes data: window K controls many development periods define cohort feature vector. cohorts observed least K periods analysed; cohorts shorter windows dropped. Increasing K captures trajectory drops recent cohorts.
code: r <- detect_cohort_regime(tri_sur, K = 12, method = "ecp") r #> <cohort_regime> #>   method      : ecp #>   value_var   : clr #>   window (K)  : elap_m 1, ..., 12 #>   cohorts     : 19 analysed (11 dropped) #>   regimes     : 2 #>   breakpoints : 24.04 #>   PC1 / PC2   : 79.8% / 13.2%

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
code: summary(r) #> Cohort regime detection summary #>   method    : ecp #>   value_var : clr #>   window    : elap_m 1, ..., 12 #>   cohorts   : 19 analysed (11 dropped) #> #> Regimes (2): #>   1: 23.04, ..., 24.03 (12 cohorts) #>   2: 24.04, ..., 24.10 (7 cohorts) #> #> Breakpoints: 24.04  r$labels #>         cohort                regime regime_id #>         <Date>                <fctr>     <int> #>  1: 2023-04-01  23.04, ..., 24.03         1 #>  2: 2023-05-01  23.04, ..., 24.03         1 #>  ... #> 13: 2024-04-01  24.04, ..., 24.10         2 #>  ...

code: plot(r)

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
text: "ecp" — preferred default. Multivariate, non-parametric, auto-detects number regimes given significance level. Slightly slower alternatives requires priori choice n_regimes. "pelt" — fast univariate change-point detection applied first principal component. May return multiple breakpoints useful trajectory variation dominated one axis (check PC1 % print() output — > 70%, PELT reliable; much lower, prefer "ecp"). "hclust" — Ward hierarchical clustering scaled feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters (default 2). Ignores chronological order best used sanity check: chronological methods locate breakpoint time t hclust produces two groups (pre-t one cluster, post-t ), shift structural rather artefact method. practice, agreement across three methods — SUR example , "ecp", "pelt", "hclust" locate 24.04 regime boundary — strong evidence real underwriting/rate shift.

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
text: want compare fixed number regimes — example, two-vs-three regime hypotheses — pass n_regimes: "ecp" "pelt", n_regimes request (algorithm return many regimes supported data). "hclust", hard cut.
code: r2 <- detect_cohort_regime(tri_sur, K = 12, method = "ecp", n_regimes = 3) summary(r2)

title: Detecting regime shifts across underwriting cohorts
text: detect_cohort_regime() preprocessing diagnostic, modification fit_lr() framework. output useful two ways: Stratified fitting: two clearly distinct regimes detected, fitting fit_lr() separately regime subset often yields sharper stable-CLR estimates pooled fit. Rate-change documentation: detected breakpoint provides data-driven anchor preprocessing recommendations outlined Limitations section companion paper (premium -leveling exposure decomposition V = C^P / r).

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
text: summary_ata() computes per-link statistics drive maturity detection: mean, median, wt — descriptive averages observed ata factors link (excluding cohorts link observed). cv — coefficient variation observed factors (relative spread, alpha-independent). f — WLS-estimated factor (volume-weighted value_from^alpha). f_se, rse — WLS standard error relative standard error. sigma — Mack residual sigma per link. n_obs, n_valid, n_inf, n_nan, valid_ratio — observation counts share finite ata factors per link.

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
code: plot(ata, type = "cv")            # CV vs ata link with maturity overlay plot(ata, type = "rse")           # RSE vs ata link plot(ata, type = "summary")       # mean / median / wt overlay per link plot(ata, type = "box")           # boxplot of observed ata per link plot(ata, type = "point")         # scatter of observed ata per link

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
text: heatmap colours cell log(ata / median(ata)) within link, column-wise colour distinguishes cohorts deviate link’s median.
code: plot_triangle(ata)                                # heatmap of observed factors plot_triangle(ata, label_style = "detail")        # factor + (loss / rp) amounts plot_triangle(ata, show_maturity = TRUE)          # overlay maturity line

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
text: maturity point development link beyond age--age factors stable enough trust chain-ladder projection. Used internally fit_lr(method = "sa") switch ED CL. find_ata_maturity() operates summary_ata() object — first build descriptive / WLS summary, probe first mature link: row per group first development link satisfying thresholds, carrying link’s full statistics. threshold arguments also stored attributes returned object. find_ata_maturity() also called internally fit_ata() fit_cl() maturity_args supplied (alpha internal summary_ata() step taken callers). Tune thresholds portfolio’s volatility profile. Tight thresholds (e.g. cv_threshold = 0.05) push maturity later; loose thresholds push earlier.
code: sm  <- summary_ata(ata, alpha = 1) mat <- find_ata_maturity(   sm,   cv_threshold    = 0.10,    # CV must be below this   rse_threshold   = 0.05,    # RSE must be below this   min_valid_ratio = 0.5,     # at least 50% finite cohorts at the link   min_n_valid     = 3L,      # at least 3 finite cohorts   min_run         = 1L       # at least 1 consecutive mature link )  print(mat) #>    cv_nm ata_from ata_to ata_link  mean median    wt    cv     f  f_se   rse  sigma  n_obs  n_valid  n_inf  n_nan  valid_ratio #> 1:   SUR        4      5      4-5  1.09   1.09  1.09  0.03  1.09  0.01  0.01   0.02      9        9      0      0         1.00 #> 2:   2CI        3      4      3-4  1.12   1.11  1.12  0.04  1.12  0.02  0.02   0.04     10       10      0      0         1.00

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
text: summary_ed() ED-side analogue summary_ata(), computing per-link statistics intensity gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k.
code: ed <- build_ed(tri, loss_var = "closs", exposure_var = "crp") sm <- summary_ed(ed, alpha = 1) head(sm)  plot(ed, type = "summary") plot(ed, type = "box") plot_triangle(ed)

title: Triangle and ata diagnostics
text: gaps development sequence suspected, inspect build_triangle(): Returns triangle_validation object one row per cohort non-consecutive development periods. empty result means triangle clean. gaps exist, options: Fix data source (preferred). Drop offending cohorts. Pass fill_gaps = TRUE build_triangle() zero-fill missing cells (use care — inflates n_obs).
code: gaps <- validate_triangle(exp, group_var = cv_nm,                           cohort_var = "uym", dev_var = "elap_m") head(gaps) #>    cv_nm        uym n_observed n_expected missing #> 1:                                       <list>

code: fit_ata(ata, alpha = 1, recent = 12)        # last 12 calendar diagonals fit_cl(tri, value_var = "closs", recent = 12) fit_lr(tri, recent = 12)

title: lossratio
text: lossratio toolkit long-term health insurance loss ratio analysis long-format experience data — one row per (cohort × dev × demographic) cell loss risk premium columns. Multi-year health policies emit loss slowly: age--age factors unstable early development, exposure (≈ risk premium) reliable anchor projection, structural shifts product redesigns, underwriting changes, regulatory reforms accumulate across cohorts. package’s defaults — stage-adaptive projection, exposure-driven early development, cohort regime detection — tuned setting. tools apply cumulative loss / exposure framework (mortality, morbidity, general claims). provides: Three aggregation frameworks experience data: cohort × dev (triangle), calendar period (calendar), portfolio total (total) Age--age (ata) exposure-driven (ed) development modeling "sa" — stage-adaptive (default): exposure-driven maturity, chain ladder "ed" — exposure-driven development periods "cl" — classical chain ladder (Mack model) Cohort regime detection structural breaks (detect_cohort_regime) Diagnostic triangle visualisations

what: Build age-to-age (ata) factors from triangle data — build_ata
title: Build age-to-age (ata) factors from triangle data — build_ata
text: Construct age--age development factors object class triangle, typically produced build_triangle(). Age--age factors calculated cumulative values closs crp. Age--age defined : $$ata_{k \k+1} = value_{k+1} / value_k$$ value_k selected cumulative metric development period k.

title: Build age-to-age (ata) factors from triangle data — build_ata
code: build_ata(   x,   value_var = "closs",   weight_var = NULL,   min_denom = 0,   drop_invalid = FALSE )

title: Build age-to-age (ata) factors from triangle data — build_ata
text: x object class triangle. value_var single cumulative metric used compute age--age. Must one "closs", "crp", "clr". weight_var optional single cumulative metric used weights WLS estimation via summary_ata(). Must one "closs", "crp", "clr", must differ value_var. Typical use weight_var = "crp" value_var = "clr", since clr values ratios carry exposure information. NULL (default), value_from used WLS weight, corresponds standard volume-weighted chain ladder. min_denom Minimum denominator required compute age--age. value_from <= min_denom, ata set NA. useful early development periods contain structural zeros (example, waiting periods life insurance). drop_invalid Logical; TRUE, rows invalid (non-finite) age--age factors dropped. Useful clean output passing summary_ata() find_ata_maturity(). FALSE (default), rows retained, preserving full triangle structure diagnostic visualisation via plot_triangle.ata().

title: Build age-to-age (ata) factors from triangle data — build_ata
text: data.frame class "ata" containing: ata_from Current development period. ata_to Next development period. value_from Current cumulative value. value_to Next cumulative value. ata Age--age factor (value_to / value_from), NA value_from <= min_denom. weight Weight values ata_from derived weight_var. present weight_var supplied. returned object carries following attributes: group_var, cohort_var, dev_var, value_var, weight_var.

what: Build a calendar-based development structure from experience data — build_calendar
title: Build a calendar-based development structure from experience data — build_calendar
text: Aggregate experience data development structure along single calendar-style period axis, including: cumulative loss cumulative risk premium, period cumulative proportions, margin cumulative margin, profit indicators, loss ratio (lr = loss / rp) cumulative loss ratio (clr = closs / crp). contrast build_triangle(), builds development structure using calendar_var × dev_var, function aggregates values one-dimensional calendar axis. loss ratio defined : $$lr = loss / rp$$ rp represents risk premium (expected loss), written premium. Proportion variables computed within calendar_var cell: loss_prop  = loss  / sum(loss) rp_prop    = rp    / sum(rp) closs_prop = closs / sum(closs) crp_prop   = crp   / sum(crp) Therefore, fixed calendar_var cell, proportions sum 1 across groups. useful examining composition calendar period across products grouping variables.

title: Build a calendar-based development structure from experience data — build_calendar

title: Build a calendar-based development structure from experience data — build_calendar
text: df data.frame containing experience data loss risk premium. group_var Column(s) used grouping (e.g., product, gender). calendar_var single calendar-like period variable defining summary axis. Typical examples include: cym (calendar year-month), cyq (calendar year-quarter), cyh (calendar year-half), cy  (calendar year), uym, uyq, uyh, uy single underwriting-period axis summarised time series rather development structure. value_var Character vector specifying value columns, typically c("loss", "rp") : loss = actual claims (observed loss), rp   = risk premium (expected loss). period_from Optional lower bound calendar_var. rows calendar_var >= period_from kept. period_to Optional upper bound calendar_var. rows calendar_var <= period_to kept. fill_gaps Logical; TRUE, zero-fill missing (group_var, calendar_var) cells every group consecutive calendar sequence (monthly, quarterly, etc. based calendar_var). Default FALSE, raises error gaps detected.

title: Build exposure-driven development data — build_ed
text: Construct exposure-driven incremental development data object class "triangle", typically produced build_triangle(). foundational data structure exposure-driven (ED) model, incremental loss modelled function cumulative exposure (risk premium) rather cumulative loss. incremental loss intensity defined : $$g_{,k} = \frac{\Delta C^L_{,k+1}}{C^P_{,k}}$$ \(\Delta C^L_{,k+1} = C^L_{,k+1} - C^L_{,k}\) incremental loss \(C^P_{,k}\) cumulative exposure (risk premium) development period \(k\).

title: Build exposure-driven development data — build_ed

title: Build exposure-driven development data — build_ed
text: x object class "triangle". loss_var single cumulative loss variable. Default "closs". exposure_var single cumulative exposure variable. Default "crp". min_exposure Minimum exposure required compute g. exposure_from <= min_exposure, g set NA. Default 0. drop_invalid Logical; TRUE, rows invalid (non-finite) g values dropped. what: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime
title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime
text: Detect structural change points sequence cohort-level development trajectories. underwriting cohort (indexed cohort_var "triangle" object) treated feature vector whose entries selected value_var observed development periods 1, ..., K. Cohorts ordered underwriting period tested structural shifts multivariate sequence. Three detection strategies supported: "ecp" Multivariate non-parametric divisive change-point detection via ecp::e.divisive(). number regimes determined data; significant breakpoints sig_level retained. Preferred number regimes known advance. "pelt" Univariate mean change-point detection via changepoint::cpt.mean() PELT algorithm applied first principal component cohort feature matrix. Fast may return multiple breakpoints. "hclust" Ward hierarchical clustering scaled cohort feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters. Ignores time ordering — useful sanity check since non-adjacent cohorts may cluster together trajectory pattern strictly chronological.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime
code: detect_cohort_regime(   x,   value_var = "clr",   K = 12L,   method = c("ecp", "pelt", "hclust"),   n_regimes = NULL,   sig_level = 0.05,   min_size = 3L,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'cohort_regime' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'cohort_regime' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.cohort_regime' print(x, ...)

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime
text: x object class "triangle". Must correspond single group (group_var single-value group_var subset). Also used S3 print() method cohort_regime objects. value_var Column name trajectory variable. Default "clr" (cumulative loss ratio). K Integer. Common development-period window used build cohort feature matrix. Cohorts fewer K observed periods dropped. Default 12. method One "ecp", "pelt", "hclust". n_regimes Integer. Number regimes force. NULL means auto-detect "ecp" "pelt"; ignored (required equal requested value) "hclust", default 2. sig_level Significance level "ecp". Default 0.05. min_size Minimum segment size "ecp". Default 3. ... Reserved future use. object object class "cohort_regime". Used S3 summary() method.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime
text: object class "cohort_regime" components: call Matched call. method Detection method used. value_var, K Trajectory variable window. cohort_var Period variable x. labels data.table one row per analysed cohort: period, regime id, regime label. breakpoints Date vector breakpoint dates (first cohort new regime; excludes initial regime start). n_regimes Number regimes detected. trajectory Cohort feature matrix (rows = cohorts, columns = development periods 1, ..., K). pca prcomp object fitted feature matrix. dropped Cohorts excluded due K window constraint.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_cohort_regime

what: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per age--age link: $$C_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot C_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2 - \alpha}\), \(w_{,k}\) either constant column supplied via weights. corresponds Mack's variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(C_{,k+1} \mid C_{,k}) \propto C_{,k}^{\alpha}\). one observation available link, factor computed directly value_to / value_from standard errors set NA. Near-zero values f_se sigma (tol) set zero avoid numerical noise essentially perfect fits.

code: .lm_ata(x, weights = 1, alpha = 1, na_rm = TRUE, tol = 1e-12)

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata

Default 1. na_rm Logical; TRUE (default), rows non-finite non-positive value_from dropped fitting. Note value_to = 0 permitted, zero cumulative values valid observations (e.g. claims yet developed early development periods). tol Non-negative numeric scalar. Values tol set zero. Default 1e-12.

title: Find ata maturity by group — find_ata_maturity
text: Identify first mature age--age (ata) link object class "ata_summary", typically produced summary_ata(). Maturity determined using combination : cv < cv_threshold rse < rse_threshold valid_ratio >= min_valid_ratio n_valid >= min_n_valid optional consecutive maturity min_run ata links cv rse must satisfied simultaneously. cv captures raw variability observed ata factors across cohorts, rse reflects precision WLS-estimated factor. what: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
text: Unified interface loss ratio projection "triangle" object. Three projection methods available: "sa" (default) Uses exposure-driven (ED) estimation maturity chain ladder (CL) maturity. maturity: age--age factors volatile, exposure-driven projection \(\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k\) anchors estimate premium volume. maturity: age--age factors stable, chain ladder projection \(C^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k\) preserves cohort's observed level. "ed" Exposure-driven development periods. future increments \(g_k \cdot C^P_k\). "cl" Chain ladder development periods. Equivalent classical Mack model. cases, exposure projected forward using chain ladder: $$\hat{C}^P_{,k+1} = f^P_k \cdot \hat{C}^P_{,k}$$

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
code: fit_lr(   x,   method = c("sa", "ed", "cl"),   loss_var = "closs",   exposure_var = "crp",   loss_alpha = 1,   exposure_alpha = 1,   delta_method = c("simple", "full"),   rho = 0,   conf_level = 0.95,   sigma_method = c("min_last2", "locf", "loglinear"),   recent = NULL,   maturity_args = NULL,   bootstrap = FALSE,   B = 1000,   seed = NULL )

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
text: x object class "triangle". method One "sa", "ed", "cl". Default "sa". loss_var Cumulative loss variable. Default "closs". exposure_var Cumulative exposure variable. Default "crp". loss_alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure loss estimation. Default 1. exposure_alpha Numeric scalar exposure chain ladder. Default 1. delta_method Method computing se_clr = SE(L/E). One : "simple" (default) se_clr = se_proj / exposure_proj, treats exposure fixed. "full" Full delta method exposure uncertainty loss-exposure correlation: $$\mathrm{Var}(L/E) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{E^2}         + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(E)}{E^4}         - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(E)}{E^3}$$ rho Numeric scalar (-1, 1); assumed correlation ultimate loss ultimate exposure. used delta_method = "full". Default 0. conf_level Confidence level used ci_lower/ci_upper cohort summary. Default 0.95. sigma_method Sigma extrapolation method. One "min_last2" (default), "locf", "loglinear". recent Optional positive integer estimation window. Default NULL. maturity_args named list forwarded find_ata_maturity(), NULL (default) skip maturity filtering. method = "sa", also determines switch point ED CL. Pass list() use defaults. bootstrap Logical; TRUE, parameter process variance derived via residual bootstrap rather analytical delta method. Default FALSE. B Integer number bootstrap replications. Used bootstrap = TRUE. Default 1000. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL.

what: lossratio: Tools for Insurance Loss Ratio Analysis — lossratio-package
title: lossratio: Tools for Insurance Loss Ratio Analysis — lossratio-package
text: toolkit analysing insurance loss ratios using risk premium-based frameworks. Supports underwriting-period, calendar-period, total aggregation, cumulative metrics, margin analysis, composition diagnostics. package provides structured workflows actuarial experience analysis, including triangle-style summaries, calendar-based views, total aggregations. also includes extensions reserving techniques chain ladder development factor estimation.

title: lossratio: Tools for Insurance Loss Ratio Analysis — lossratio-package
text: core loss ratio defined : $$lr = loss / rp$$ rp represents risk premium, written premium. package designed practical actuarial workflows, emphasising consistency across summary functions compatibility data.table-based pipelines.

title: lossratio: Tools for Insurance Loss Ratio Analysis — lossratio-package

title: Summarise age-to-age factor statistics — summary_ata
text: wt f weighted averages observed ata factors, differ weights assigned observations included: wt Volume-weighted mean: \(wt = \sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\). Computed rows value_from value_to finite, including rows either value zero. Independent alpha. f WLS-estimated factor. rows value_from > 0 used, since value_from = 0 causes numerical issues WLS weights (\(w = value\_from^{\alpha}\)). alpha = 2, f wt numerically equivalent (assuming zero value_from rows). alpha \ne 2, diverge. Therefore wt f can differ two reasons: Zero exclusion: rows value_from = 0 included wt excluded f. typically affects early development periods cohorts yet accumulated claims. Alpha effect: alpha \ne 2, WLS weights differ volume weights used wt, leading different estimates. Comparing wt f can help diagnose whether choice alpha materially affects estimated factor.

title: Summarise age-to-age factor statistics — summary_ata
text: coefficient variation defined : $$cv = \frac{SD(f_k)}{\bar{f}_k}$$ \(f_k\) individual observed ata values link \(k\) \(\bar{f}_k\) arithmetic mean. cv reflects relative spread observed factors across cohorts, regardless exposure scale. used find_ata_maturity() one criteria determining maturity point.

title: Summarise age-to-age factor statistics — summary_ata
text: relative standard error defined : $$rse = \frac{SE(\hat{f}_k)}{\hat{f}_k}$$ \(SE(\hat{f}_k)\) standard error WLS-estimated factor. Unlike cv, treats cohorts equally, rse gives weight cohorts larger exposures (via WLS weights). small rse indicates WLS estimate precise, tends occur : (1) many cohorts, (2) exposures large, (3) observed ata values consistent across cohorts.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle

Non-consecutive cohorts produce non-consecutive age--age links downstream (e.g., 14 -> 17 instead 14 -> 15), breaks summary_ata() / summary_ed() key uniqueness causes cartesian joins fit_lr(). function inspects raw data without modifying . text: Experience class: as_experience(), is_experience(), check_experience(), add_experience_period(). Exposure-driven: build_ed(), fit_ed(), summary_ed(). Projection: fit_cl() (chain ladder, basic / mack), fit_lr() (loss-ratio sa / ed / cl methods). Diagnostics: detect_cohort_regime() (regime detection across underwriting cohorts), backtest() (calendar-diagonal hold-, supports fit_cl fit_lr).
